items.title: The Gospel According to John (I-XII). New York: Doubleday, 1966
items.author: Brown, Raymond E
items.why: Brown explores the Johannine themes of fulfillment of Old Testament prophecies through Jesus.
items.description: Brown explores the Johannine themes of fulfillment of Old Testament prophecies through Jesus.
items.citation: Brown, Raymond E. *The Gospel According to John (I-XII)*. New York: Doubleday, 1966.

items.title: The Gospel of John: A Commentary. Philadelphia: Westminster Press, 1971
items.author: Bultmann, Rudolf
items.why: Bultmann discusses the theological implications of Jesus as the Word made flesh in relation to the Old Testament.
items.description: Bultmann discusses the theological implications of Jesus as the Word made flesh in relation to the Old Testament.
items.citation: Bultmann, Rudolf. *The Gospel of John: A Commentary*. Philadelphia: Westminster Press, 1971.

items.title: Let the Reader Understand: A Theological Introduction to the Gospel of John. New York: T&T Clark, 2006
items.author: Fowler, Robert M
items.why: Fowler discusses the theological implications of belief and love in John's narrative.
items.description: Fowler discusses the theological implications of belief and love in John's narrative.
items.citation: Fowler, Robert M. *Let the Reader Understand: A Theological Introduction to the Gospel of John*. New York: T&T Clark, 2006.

items.title: Paul and Empire: Religion and Power in Roman Imperial Society. Trinity Press International, 1997
items.author: Horsley, Richard A
items.why: Horsley discusses the socio-political implications of Jesus' rejection by Israel.
items.description: Horsley discusses the socio-political implications of Jesus' rejection by Israel.
items.citation: Horsley, Richard A. *Paul and Empire: Religion and Power in Roman Imperial Society*. Trinity Press International, 1997.

items.title: New Testament Theology. London: SCM Press, 1964
items.author: Käsemann, Ernst
items.why: Käsemann analyzes the implications of Israel's rejection of Jesus within the context of New Testament theology.
items.description: Käsemann analyzes the implications of Israel's rejection of Jesus within the context of New Testament theology.
items.citation: Käsemann, Ernst. *New Testament Theology*. London: SCM Press, 1964.

items.title: The Theology of the Gospel of John. Cambridge: Cambridge University Press, 1992

items.why: Lindars discusses the resurrection's theological implications in John's Gospel.
items.description: Lindars discusses the resurrection's theological implications in John's Gospel.

items.title: The Gospel According to John. Grand Rapids: Eerdmans, 1971

items.why: Morris provides insights into the themes of purification and new birth in John's Gospel.
items.description: Morris provides insights into the themes of purification and new birth in John's Gospel.

items.title: Knowing God. Downers Grove: InterVarsity Press, 1973
items.author: Packer, J.I
items.why: Packer explores the significance of Jesus' resurrection in the context of Christian faith.
items.description: Packer explores the significance of Jesus' resurrection in the context of Christian faith.

items.title: The Gospel According to St. John. New York: Crossroad, 1980

items.why: Schnackenburg examines the shift from law to love in the context of John's Gospel.
items.description: Schnackenburg examines the shift from law to love in the context of John's Gospel.
